sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive systems estimate outcomes, risks, categories, or interventions from structured records using classical, probabilistic, and neural models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: PRML, PML, the deep-learning book, and NIST collectively support structured-record prediction with classical, probabilistic, neural, risk, and decision-support methods.

sentence_reviews.text: They remain the dominant practical boundary for credit, maintenance, operations, public services, and enterprise decision support.

sentence_reviews.text: Leakage, confounding, feedback loops, missing data, subgroup error, and changing business processes can invalidate results.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: PML/PRML and NIST support concerns around missing data, uncertainty, bias, changing context, and evaluation risk; feedback loops and business-process drift are reasonable synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Linear models & generalized linear models as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Linear and generalized linear models are core tabular predictive methods in PRML/PML.

sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates k-NN & Naive Bayes as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: k-NN and Naive Bayes are standard classical predictive methods for structured features.

sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Support vector & kernel machines as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: SVMs and kernel methods are standard tabular classifiers/regressors; common strength is defensible.

sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Tree ensembles are common structured-data predictive models.

sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Gaussian processes as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Gaussian processes are supported probabilistic predictors, though not universal; common strength is plausible.

sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Calibration, uncertainty & risk evaluation as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Risk scoring and decision support require calibration and evaluation; optional strength is supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates PCA, ICA, NMF & manifold reduction as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dimensionality reduction is a plausible optional preprocessing/analysis dependency for structured records.

sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Single-layer neural classifiers are classical predictive models, optional in modern tabular systems.

sentence_reviews.text: Tabular predictive analytics & decision support integrates Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: NIST and risk decision-support contexts support fairness and subgroup analysis as an optional dependency.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support has an `applies` dependency on Self-organizing maps & competitive learning with `optional` strength.

edge_reviews.notes: Linear and generalized linear models are core tabular predictive methods in PRML/PML.
k-NN and Naive Bayes are standard classical predictive methods for structured features.
SVMs and kernel methods are standard tabular classifiers/regressors; common strength is defensible.
Tree ensembles are common structured-data predictive models.
Gaussian processes are supported probabilistic predictors, though not universal; common strength is plausible.
Risk scoring and decision support require calibration and evaluation; optional strength is supported.
Dimensionality reduction is a plausible optional preprocessing/analysis dependency for structured records.
Single-layer neural classifiers are classical predictive models, optional in modern tabular systems.
NIST and risk decision-support contexts support fairness and subgroup analysis as an optional dependency.
